major.meaning: A change that requires operator action before the repository contract or managed platform can continue to function correctly.
triggers: Breaking API publication contract change in config/api-publication.json
OpenBao secret path or format change consumed at service startup
One-time manual operator action that cannot be automated safely

minor.meaning: A backward-compatible capability addition or new operator-visible workflow.
triggers: New service, dashboard, workflow, or CLI command added
New runbook-backed operational surface added without breaking an existing contract

patch.meaning: A correction that does not materially change the operating contract.
triggers: Safe package or image pin refresh with no operator-facing contract change

breaking_change_criteria.description: Removing a managed service from the service capability catalog is a major release.
Removing or renaming an existing published API or webhook contract is a major release.
Changing the path or startup format of a secret consumed by a managed service is a major release.
Changing the declared IP addressing or other deterministic guest identity inputs is a major release.
If the upgrade requires a one-time manual operator action that cannot be automated safely, the release is major.

rules.notes: All 0.x repository releases remain additive unless explicitly called out as major.
Version 1.0.0 is a stabilisation milestone with a zero-step upgrade guide.
Follow the dedicated v2 upgrade guide before applying the release.
